name: github-sync-4step (real docs/content/features/github-sync.mdx, D5)
source:
<Steps>

<Step>
    ### Open the clone dialog

    From the Navigator window, click **Clone from GitHub**. You can open the navigator window from the editor by clicking on your project name in the bottom left and then clicking **Switch project**.
</Step>

<Step>
    ### Sign into GitHub

    If you are already signed in with the GitHub CLI, Open Knowledge will use those credentials by default. If not, click **Sign into GitHub** and complete the device authentication flow.
</Step>

<Step>
    ### Choose a repository

    Paste a repository URL or `owner/repo` shorthand. You can also filter and select from a list of repositories your account can access.
</Step>

<Step>
    ### Open the project

    When the clone completes, the cloned project opens in a new editor window and the Navigator closes. Re-summon the Navigator from inside the editor via the sidebar Switch Project pill, the File menu, or the Command Palette.
</Step>
</Steps>
notes: M4 — the production doc's flush-left <Step> shape with 4-space-indented ### heading + paragraph children. RED on HEAD post-#1904 (211->218), GREEN under step-7g. This is the canonical fidelity fixture (D5); current i13 was a 2-Step reduction.

name: m2-blank-inside (heading-less, indented Step, blank before body)
source:
<Steps>

  <Step>

    Install the package using your package manager of choice.

  </Step>

</Steps>
notes: M2 — a bare paragraph child under a blank line inside an indented <Step>. The blank defeats 7f on HEAD (44->43). GREEN under step-7g.

name: heading-less flush-left multi-Step
source:
<Steps>

<Step>

Clone the repository to your local machine.

</Step>

<Step>

Install the project dependencies.

</Step>

<Step>

Start the development server.

</Step>

</Steps>
notes: Heading-less, flush-left <Step>, paragraph-only children — the simplest still-broken-on-HEAD shape.

name: m3-two-sibling-Steps-blocks
source:
## First procedure

<Steps>

<Step>

Do the first thing.

</Step>

<Step>

Do the second thing.

</Step>

</Steps>

## Second procedure

<Steps>

<Step>

Do another first thing.

</Step>

<Step>

Do another second thing.

</Step>

</Steps>
notes: M3 — two separate <Steps> blocks in one doc (124->125 on HEAD, direction flipped by #1904). GREEN under step-7g.

name: tabs-tab with indented code-ish body
source:
<Tabs>

<Tab value="npm">

    Run npm install to add the package.

</Tab>

<Tab value="pnpm">

    Run pnpm add to install the package.

</Tab>

</Tabs>
notes: Tabs/Tab container with an attribute on the child tag — tag-name independent of <Steps>.

name: depth-3 nested Steps > Step > Tabs > Tab
source:

<Step>
    ### Configure the package manager

    <Tabs>

    <Tab value="npm">

    Use npm for this step.

    </Tab>

    <Tab value="yarn">

    Use yarn for this step.

    </Tab>

    </Tabs>
</Step>

</Steps>
notes: Depth-3 nested containers (Steps > Step > Tabs > Tab) with a heading sibling.

name: steps-with-fenced-code-child (CommonMark 4.4 hazard intersection)
source:
<Steps>

<Step>
    ### Add the config

    ```ts
    export const config = {
      enabled: true,
    };
    ```
</Step>

</Steps>
notes: A fenced-code block inside a <Step> — the fence interior must be byte-preserved (never folded) while the container boundary blanks fold. Intersects CommonMark 4.4 indented-code handling.
